title: Scope rebuilds with const widgets and state placement
description: Place state as low as possible and mark static subtrees const so a change rebuilds only what depends on it.
body: Use when a small change causes a large or janky rebuild. Do not hoist all state to a top-level widget that rebuilds the entire tree, and do not omit const on widgets that never change. Actions: keep state in the smallest widget that needs it, extract const subtrees so the framework skips rebuilding them, and use targeted listeners (Selector/Consumer, ValueListenableBuilder) so only dependent widgets rebuild. Validation signals include the Flutter DevTools rebuild counter showing only the intended widgets rebuild on a change. Risk boundary: over-broad rebuilds cause visible jank on lists and animations; misplaced state turns a local update into a whole-screen rebuild, so verify with the rebuild profiler, not by eye.
